meta.feed_title: Latest World News on Fox News

items.title: US adversaries China, North Korea strengthening ties as Xi, Kim set to begin talks
items.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping arrived in Pyongyang for a rare state visit, set to meet with North Korea's Kim Jong Un as both nations seek to restore their traditional alliance.

items.title: Hezbollah's secret 'kill, wound and maim' bomb network exposed as Israel strikes Beirut
items.summary: Israel launched retaliatory airstrikes on Hezbollah command centers in Beirut after rockets were fired into northern Israel, marking a major escalation.

items.title: Iran admits extraordinary new detail in Khamenei strike, Trump offered 'way out': expert
items.summary: Iran's foreign minister reveals new details about the strike that killed Supreme Leader Khamenei, confirming U.S.-Israeli precision targeting.

items.title: Suspected Hamas terrorist arrested in Greece for allegedly plotting attack on Israeli cruise ship
items.summary: A suspected Hamas terrorist reportedly granted asylum in Greece was arrested in Crete for allegedly plotting an attack on an Israeli cruise ship.

items.title: Americans travel to Pakistan to free Christians trapped in modern-day slavery: 'God's hand was in it'
items.summary: Aaron Hutchings says he was shocked to see children making bricks under the hot sun in Pakistan to pay off family debts that had accumulated over generations.

items.title: NATO's eastern flank races to rearm as Trump pressure exposes Western Europe's defense gap
items.summary: Eastern European NATO allies closest to Russia are spending the most on defense, while Western Europe faces pressure to meet the alliance's 5% GDP goal.

items.title: Pro-US conservative faces leftist in Peru's high-stakes presidential runoff
items.summary: Keiko Fujimori and Roberto Sánchez face off in Peru's pivotal presidential runoff, a race that could determine the country's alignment with Washington or a leftward shift.

items.title: Pope Leo XIV jokes young Spaniards would pick Bad Bunny over him during Madrid visit this weekend
items.summary: Pope Leo XIV joked that young Spaniards might choose Bad Bunny over him during his Madrid visit, but an estimated 500,000 gathered for a vigil.

items.title: Spearfisherman killed by suspected 15-foot shark after third fatal attack in less than a month
items.summary: A 35-year-old spearfisherman was killed by a suspected 15-foot shark off Western Australia, marking the third fatal attack there in under a month.

items.title: World War II veteran reveals 1-word feeling before D-Day 82 years later
items.summary: WWII veteran Arthur Rose read a letter written days after D-Day at an 82nd anniversary ceremony in Normandy, France, recounting the historic invasion.

items.title: Jeff Bartos says UN reform is no longer an 'oxymoron' after $570M in cuts
items.summary: Jeff Bartos details $570 million in U.N. budget cuts and 2,900 eliminated positions as the Trump administration pushes historic reform efforts.

items.title: Raúl Castro makes first public appearance since Trump administration charged him with murder
items.summary: Raúl Castro made his first public appearance since Trump charged him with murder over the 1996 shoot-down of Brothers to the Rescue planes in Cuba.

items.title: US, Shield of the Americas condemn 'ongoing efforts' to overthrow Bolivia's elected president amid unrest
items.summary: The U.S. and the Shield of the Americas countries condemned ongoing efforts to overthrow Bolivia's elected President Rodrigo Paz amid weeks of mass protests.

items.title: Zelenskyy issues open letter to Putin proposing meeting as US 'fully focused' on Iran
items.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy proposes meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin to negotiate end of years-long Russia-Ukraine war.

items.title: Pete Hegseth warns narco-terrorists as US backs Bolivia's government amid coup warnings
items.summary: War Secretary Pete Hegseth says the U.S. remains committed to defending Bolivia's government amid coup warnings and mass unrest over fuel prices.

items.title: As Hezbollah rejects truce, families on Israel's northern border describe life under fire
items.summary: Residents of Israel's northern border describe life under what they call "the ceasefire war," with sirens and explosions despite agreements.

items.title: Canada's Carney pledges action on antisemitism amid backlash over new anti-hate council members
items.summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney's new council to combat antisemitism draws backlash over members critics say are hostile to Israel and Jewish concerns.

items.title: Sherpa missing for a week on Everest found crawling toward base camp after his family begins funeral rites
items.summary: Rescuers call it a miracle after a Sherpa guide survived nearly a week alone on Mount Everest without food, water or supplemental oxygen in the death zone.

items.title: Albino buffalo nicknamed 'Donald Trump' becomes sensation at Bangladesh's national zoo for its blond hair
items.summary: A rare albino buffalo nicknamed "Donald Trump" has become a sensation at Bangladesh's national zoo thanks to its blond hair resembling the president.

items.title: Trump expands Cuba sanctions beyond US companies in major crackdown on foreign enablers
items.summary: Trump administration rolls out unprecedented secondary sanctions on Cuba targeting foreign firms doing business with military-linked conglomerate GAESA.

items.title: Bodycam footage fuels backlash after police handcuff dying stabbing victim
items.summary: Bodycam footage shows English police handcuffing 18-year-old Henry Nowak after he said he was stabbed and could not breathe moments before dying.

items.title: US ally Kuwait condemns 'brutal and ongoing Iranian attacks' after airport was hit
items.summary: Kuwait has condemned "the brutal and ongoing Iranian attacks using ballistic missiles and drones," saying Kuwait International Airport had been targeted.

items.title: Israeli official says EU sanctions reveal antisemitism hiding behind 'socially acceptable mask'
items.summary: Israel's Deputy Foreign Minister accuses the EU of disguising antisemitism as anti-Zionism after sanctions hit Israeli civil society organizations.

items.title: Iranians speak out over possible Trump-regime deal
items.summary: Iranians inside Iran express fear and frustration that a potential U.S.-Iran deal could leave the Islamic Republic intact despite economic devastation.

items.title: US ally answers Trump's call on Strait of Hormuz: 'Part of a diplomatic effort'
items.summary: Romania allowed access to military bases for air refueling and defensive activities amid U.S.-Iran tensions, its foreign minister tells Fox News Digital.
